description: parse_request_size wraps std::stoi in try/catch to absorb malformed-input exceptions; parse_internal_priority and parse_internal_weight let stoi propagate.

findings.rationale: parse_request_size wraps std::stoi in try/catch; the other two parsers let the exception propagate. The guard is load-bearing on the request path: the header value is attacker-controllable, and an unhandled invalid_argument/out_of_range crashes the worker process. Unifying onto a shared parse() that drops the catch re-introduces a DoS vector.
